game.short_text: A victorian-undead roguelike action game.
game.title: Crumbs of Vengeance

details.title: Crumbs of Vengeance
details.description:
An entry made for the Brackeys Game Jam 2025.2.
Controls:
E to InteractWASD to moveShift to DashLMB to shootR to reload
Concept:
This is a roguelike adventure-action game set in a fantasy Victorian world, one where our modern technology coexists with the nature of the historic era. In this world, the undead and humans live side by side, with conflicts happening from time to time. One day, Meredith woke up to find her biscuits gone! Surely, they couldn't have gotten far.

About this:
This was my first time making a game solo, my first time dealing with FMod, my first time making art and animations for a full game, and my first time doing everything. I had my friend help me with the main character animations and one of the weapons. I am a video game composer by trade, and I don't usually create games myself. But I decided to give this one a try. I don't think it turned out too badly.
I apologize for the inconsistent lighting and artstyle - I never made art before.
Credits:
Code, Music, Audio direction, Game design, VFX by me.
Art & Animation by me and my friend RazBecause.
SFX from Krotos Studio Pro, Envato Elements and Epidemic Sounds.
Music was done in Cubase 14 using various VSTs like Cinematic Studio Series, Storm Choir, CinePerc, BBCSO Pro and more. 
Used Unity 6.2 and Aseprite.
Used FMOD.

game.short_text: Deck-building roguelike black jack game about baking biscuits.
game.title: Bake Jack

details.title: Bake Jack
details.description:
Bake Jack is a deck-building roguelike baking game made for Brackeys Game Jam 2025.2 with the theme "risk it for the biscuit".
Play as Jack the baker, a bear who loves cookies almost as much as his ravenous clientele! Grow your arsenal of unique ingredients to make tastier cookies through a blackjack-based baking system. Be sure not to go overboard and ruin the cookie; Your clients are feisty raccoons, and they will attack when they get hungry!
Create powerful decks, learn to make the most of your limited cards, and stay one step ahead of the growing demands in Bake Jack.
How to play:

Gameplay notes:
Click on a cookie to select it and then clic either a) a raccoon to feed it, b) Jake's portrait to heal yourself, c) the spot in the baking tray where the cookie was to drop it where it was without using it.There are 16 predefined waves and then the game switches to an endless mode with increasing difficulty.There are 29 upgrades/modifiers to buy leading to a huge amount of possible deck combinations. They all come with their pros and cons. Some times the best choice is to stick to your current deck or roll different upgrades.Set your name in the settings to participate in the leaderboard.There are some powerful ingredient combinations:Heat = 21, 2x multiplierAll of the same suit, 1.5x multiplierAnd more hidden recipes!
Credits:
Game designed by Josep Valls, Sina Scribblecraft and JoshOriginal artwork by Sina Scribblecraft: https://sinascribblecraft.itch.io/Original soundtrack and SFX by Josh: https://josh011.itch.io/Particle effects from https://kenney.nl/assets/particle-pack (CC0)Shield icon from https://www.vecteezy.com/vector-art/43193071-wooden-shield-model-design-illustra...Additional icons from https://kenney.nl/assetsBalsamiqSans and BubblegumSans from Google FontsFree leaderboard addon and backend services by SilentWolf: https://silentwolf.com/A project template with basic settings and menus was derived from: https://github.com/crystal-bit/godot-game-template/tree/godot-3No AI was used in the development of this game
QA and special thanks:DonRogelioOtter841Cookie recipes:
There are some special recipes that provide additional cookie satisfaction strength:
Great cookie 2x: A cookie with heat value equals 21Perfect cookie 2x: A cookie with heat value of 21 with exactly two cardsLarge cookie 4x: A cookie made with 5 or more cardsNutty/Fruty/Chocolatey 1.5x: A cookie with a single suit of cardsRainbow 4x: A cookie with 4 or more suits of cards
The recipes stack. For example a cookie with an ace (11) and a face (10) of chocolate gets a satisfaction value of 21x2x2x1.5=126.
Deck modifiers:
The deck modifiers can be purchased from the shop with the currency earned by the raccoons. Modifiers are randomly offered, keep an eye for these specials:
Add spice cards to your deck. Spice cards do not have a satisfaction value but spiced cookies can be used to permanently increase your health.Add fireproof cards to your deck. Fireproof cards do not have a satisfaction but will prevent overheating (busting) once, allowing you to bake larger cookies going over the max heat of 21.

Updates:

Check our devblog for more information about our first major update here.

Ginger's Table is a coin flipping game where you duel an opponent. As far as I know it's an original game concept. This game has been created for Brackey's Game Jam 2025 with theme "risk it for the biscuit".
Note: Press Esc (When not in dialogue) to open the pause menu if you want to disable the CRT filter. It appears MUCH worse online.

The design includes a roguelike system where you can choose some coins to take from opponents you beat, in order to build your coin purse and create a "deck". However this feature is currently disabled as I didn't manage to debug it during the timeframe, so its just the one battle for now.

details.title: Alice in Biscuitland
details.description:
Plummeting headfirst into Biscuitland, Alice is catapulted into a series of increasingly challenging face-offs with Biscuitland's notorious inhabitants. With only biscuits as her weapons, she faces a tricky decision: should she attempt to upgrade her biscuits by dipping them in her tea, or should she shy away from the risk of them sinking, and being lost forever?
In this game, you play as Alice, exploring the Biscuit-y (and somewhat familiar) realm while assembling your arsenal of biscuit weapons, in preparation to defeat Biscuitland's most infamous villain...
HOW TO PLAY
Click and drag biscuits to move them. Play biscuits by dropping them on the intended recipient - the opponent, yourself (your hands), or your teacup.
TURN ORDER:
The game is played in turns. On your turn, you pay 3 of your 5 biscuits. Then, the opponent plays up to 3 biscuits. You can see what biscuits the opponent will play on their next turn on the left of the screen.
THIRST:
Each biscuit has a different effect. Some biscuits inflict Thirst on the opponent, forcing them to drink their tea. You can see how much tea the opponent has on top of their teacup, which is to the right of the opponent. If the opponent runs out of tea, you have won the battle!
Equally, opponents will try to inflict Thirst on you, forcing you to drink your tea. If you run out of tea, you lost the battle!
DEFENSE:
Some biscuits inflict Defense. Both you and the opponent have a Defense bar, which is on top of your teacups. Biscuits that increase Defense add to this Defense bar. The Defense bar acts as a buffer, reducing the Thirst inflicted from an opponent's biscuit by the Defense level. Any excess Thirst is then deducted from your tea.
So, you will (most of the time) want to drag biscuits that inflict Thirst to the opponent, and biscuits that increase Defense to yourself.
DUNKING BISCUITS IN TEA:
Finally, before you play any biscuit, you can choose to Dunk it in your tea. The benefit is that its effect is upgraded, but the risk is it might sink in the tea! The thermometer on the left of your tea tells you the odds of biscuits sinking if you try to Dunk them. The higher the level in the thermometer, the greater the chance of your biscuit sinking in the tea . These odds may change...
MODIFIERS:
Some biscuits have effects which last across turns. 
(Bug: the modifiers are meant to appear on the top right, but mysteriously do not.)
POWERUPS:
Your powerups are shown on the bottom right, and the opponent's powerups are to the left of them, and purple.
Thirst powerup: Increases the Thirst inflicted by any Thirst biscuit by the number on the powerup. At the start, this value is 0.
Defense powerup: Increases the Defense provided by any Defense biscuit by the number on the powerup. At the start, this value is 0.




This game is designed for Desktop. Doesn't work on Mobile.

game.short_text: Biting off as much biscuits before rolling them to you customers! A silly and simple yet challange physic-base game.
game.title: Fairy Edible Deliveries

details.title: Fairy Edible Deliveries
details.description:
Fairy Edible Deliveries
Created in 7 days for Brackeys Game Jam 2025.2. Theme: Risk it for the biscuit

Being hungry on the job is no joke, especially when you are delivering biscuits! How many nibble* can you sneak in before making that yummy biscuit not rollable?  
Note and Current known issues 
 Bite cancelling might not work on Linux (Using left mouse button + ctrl might work)
Version History

Version 1.0.6 (Latest Release)

Added support for touch devicesAdded life for all game modesAdded the ability to skip intro and score screensFixed the saving systemVarious minor fixes



Version 1.0.5

Initial Release


Team
Kelvin - Design, Programmer, Art
fanngaipui - Design, Audio, Playtest

Synopsis
You are a fairy tasked with delivering biscuits to your downhill neighbour. But you are hungry! Knowing your client can't even tell square from circle, you decided to have a few bites yourself. Be careful though, as biting in more and more makes the biscuit more difficult to roll and could not get there. At that point your client will definitely notice! Are you good enough to cheat on the 5 biscuits job, or even cooler, master the 9 biscuits job?

Gameplay
The gameplay loop is simple. You've got a fixed amount of time each level to bite at your heart's desire. You can charge to create a deeper bite, and can also cancel the charge at any time. Once you are happy, you can trigger a launch and send the biscuit down the hill. The biscuit will also launch automatically if the timer reaches zerol. The level is considered cleared if the biscuit reaches its destination, and you've eaten enough of the biscuit.
The game contains two difficulty modes, where the easy mode consists of 5 biscuits with an easier cleared condition. Hard mode is unlocked after beating easy mode, and consists of 9 biscuits instead. The game concludes after hard mode is beaten.
Theme interpretation
"Risk it for the biscuit" as "risk", where you are basically risking failing the game (not delivering the biscuit) by aiming for more points, and "biscuit", literally where the game is about delivering biscuit.
Controls
[Space], [Left Mouse Button]: Press to charge, release to take the bite[Shift], [Right Mouse Button]: Launch the biscuit prematurely, or cancel the charge if charging

A submission for the Brackeys 2025.2 Game Jam!
  
    About the Game
    Welcome to Slap Dungeon! ... a game jam entry born from the Brackeys 2025.2 challenge. 
    Your objective is simple: collect as many delicious biscuits as possible. But beware! This isn't just a casual stroll through a biscuit paradise. Danger lurks in the form of relentless enemies who will chase you down and… well, slap you! Don't worry though, you're not defenseless; you can slap them right back!

    If you'd like to see more of this game come to life, consider donating! As a token of my gratitude, anyone who donates will receive a final game key for FREE upon release.
  
  
    Behind the Art
    The charming artwork you see in Slap Dungeon! was lovingly crafted by my 8-year-old son. He drew everything on paper, and I then transferred his wonderful creations onto the screen. We have so many more ideas, from special "slapping" hands to new biscuit types ... we're really excited to make this happen!

details.title: Risk It for The Biscuit
details.description:
TRIGGER WARNING: Risk It for The Biscuit features flashing lights and loud noises

Welcome to Risk It for The Biscuit, a turn-based tabletop strategy game heavily reliant on elements of Risk & Reward.
Compete with your Opponent as contestants on a game show where you have to risk it all for a chance to win $100,000 in cash prizes. You may be motivated to win for other reasons as well, as something about this show seems strangely off...
 


Take turns dipping your cookies in glasses of Milk and Acid and risk losing points. Risk It for The Biscuit is a refreshing take on the turn-based guessing gameplay, inspired by games like Buckshot Roulette.
 


Not everything in this game is based on chance. Power-ups offer a way for you to strategise and gain the upper hand over your opponent. However, using a Power-up is still tied to the element of Risk to some extent. Use them wisely!

For the best visual experience, I strongly recommend downloading the game instead of playing it in your browser.
 
KNOWN ISSUES
There is a roughly 5% chance that the game gets softlocked at one turn during a single match.On a rare occasion, the opponent will make multiple decisions during the third round.
The opponent will sometimes skip his own turn.
The third round sometimes occurs twice.

game.short_text: Steal some dog treats, but not too many!
game.title: Dog Treats

details.title: Dog Treats
details.description:
Why
I made this game for Brackeys Game Jam 2025.2 "Risk it for the Biscuit".
The idea of using a literal approach to the theme was funny, so I decided to recreate a real life risk and reward dynamic that actually occurs between me and my dog:pretend to steal his biscuits to see his funny reaction (the risk is that he could bite me...).
Design
Dog Treats is all based on math, chances, risk and reward.
You basically always want to steal as many biscuits as possible, but you don't know how many nor what kind of biscuits will come out of the Dog Treats box.
So, it is up to you to decide if you have to stop or take the risk of stealing some more.
Be careful though, because you have to leave something for the dog too, otherwise he'll get angry.
Disclaimers
This is my first game jam!I'm not new to game development, but I wanted to test my skills on something new.
Unfortunately there is no way to change the audio in the game, so I suggest to adjust the volume from your mixer.Speaking of audio, the sounds are the only thing not entirely made by me.They come from Mixkit and Pixabay.
Special thanks to my girlfriend and friends who have supported me this week (and yes also thanks to my dog...).
P.S. Try to beat my record, I posted it between the screenshots.Hope you enjoy it!Extract the zip folder and execute the application with the Unreal icon.
Then you will probably need to authorise the execution of the file.

game.short_text: A Roguelike Deckbuilding Game featuring Dogs
game.title: Bet the House

details.title: Bet the House
details.description:
Bet The House: How to Play
Goal: Build a hand of Dogs worth enough Victory Points (VP) to clear the VP Goal. The VP Goal increases every turn and each Ante.

Setup
Start with a randomized deck of 12 dogs, and a starting hand of 5.
You have Biscuits to pay each card’s Biscuit Cost to retain it between turns or to pay for your dogs’ effects.

Your turn.
You have the option to Take Actions OR Play Hand. If your Total Victory Points falls within or exceeds the Goal Range at the top of the screen, Play Hand to advance to the next Ante.


Be Careful! You won’t know exactly how many Victory Points are required to win. It will be a random number within the Goal Range. 

If you choose not to Play Hand you may Take Actions, but the Goal Range will increase!

You have 5 actions you can spend to:

Discard And Draw: Selects Dogs to Discard, then Draw replacements.
Merge duplicates: Combine 2 identical Dogs into 1; add their values together and add +1 VP.


Dog Effects:

Some Dogs have effects that trigger immediately when you Discard them, when you Draw them or when you Merge them.


Effects that raise or lower a Dog’s VP are permanent unless stated otherwise.

Some Dogs also have Retain effects that are active the turn after they are retained. Their effects last for the duration of that turn.

End of Turn
Retain Phase: Choose Dogs to Retain, their Retain Cost will be subtracted from your biscuit total.
VP Requirement Increases: The VP needed to clear the level rises each turn by a random amount within a range.

Winning a Level
Play your hand when you think your VP is high enough.
If your total VP is higher than the hidden VP goal, you clear the Ante and gain a Dog Reward.
Reward: Gain 3 new cards that you may add to your deck and more Biscuits.
Additional Rules**
You can sell your collar or other belongings to gain extra biscuits, this can keep you afloat a bit longer to clear levels. Be Careful! Your wife would kill you if you lost the house!

game.short_text: A deadly onboarding test with a single bullet

details.title: One-Time  Trigger
details.description:
▶Story◀

 - You participated in a cruel test. In order to get a job at Providence Company, in a strange enclosed containment facility, you must use the only bullet to accurately shoot the only imposter among the seven patients. Mistakenly shooting other contained creatures will trigger a total wipeout, and hesitation will make you the prey of the imposter.


▶Game Process & Clearance Conditions◀

▶Your main objective

            correctly identify and shoot the impostor among the 7 contained entities.


▶Standard Process

Exploration Phase: Enter each room (some rooms require specific items to enter).
Interaction Phase: Engage in dialogue with entities, listen to their requests, lies, or crazy whispers. Click to investigate items in the room and collect key props.
Inference Phase: Integrate all information, compare testimonies, identify logical contradictions and behavioral loopholes, and pinpoint the impostor.
Execution Phase: Load the bullets into the pistol found in Room 2, proceed to the room where you believe the android is located, and pull the trigger of your one-time-use pistol.


▶Passing conditions
▶True Ending (Completion):

      Successfully shoot the impostor. Successfully leave through the facility exit, and you have passed the test (but the truth about the company is just beginning to unfold...).
▶Bad Ending:

Accidentally killing any real entity. This triggers a catastrophic containment breach, and you will meet your end in despair.

▶Other endings:

leaving bullets unused, being killed by a doppelganger, or triggering the death conditions of other entities during exploration... Explore these endings to piece together the complete story.

▶Game Controls: 
Save Point:

      You can save your game here 

Props - Interaction:
 You can click on certain areas in the scene to try interacting with objects and patients, while also ensuring your own safety.
Props - Pickup: 
Some props can be picked up and equipped, and interactions may change after being equipped.
Leaving/Entering the Room:
 .

 You can click to leave the room in approximately this area
 .

click on the door in the corridor to enter the room.


▶Additional Explanation◀

Due to limited time, we're sorry that we've only completed 4 rooms. The 4 rooms in the distance are temporarily not open, but we highly value your suggestions. Please feel free to leave us a message.
We apologize for the loss of some interactive prompts due to the inability of the HTML version to display mouse cursors.
Swipe down to download the full PC version.

details.title: Stuffed Guardians
details.description:
Stuffed Guardians
You wake up in the middle of the night, and something is not right. Your stuffed toys have come to life and woken you up as they want cookies.
The familiar rooms of your home are now filled with shadows and unsettling noises. Before heading to the kitchen, choose one stuffed guardian to carry with you. Each has a unique ability.

Created for Brackeys Game Jam 2025.2
Ranked out of 2,301 entries:
#3 Audio 🏆
#29 Visuals
#43 Overall

Play on mobile
The web build automatically detects mobile devices and enables the on-screen joystick. 
For the best experience, download Stuffed Guardians on Google Play.

Pick your stuffed guardian
Each stuffed guardian has a distinct voice-acted personality and grants different abilities to survive the night, as you encounter 4 unique monsters throughout your haunted home, each with their own battle theme.

details.description:
Dive into the unknown depths. Explore and search for loot to bring back with you and upgrade your tools so your next dive will go even further. Eventually you might even find the greatest treasure of them all.

How to play:
Grab your bag and dive deep searching for loot. Return back to the diving bell to sell and buy new things. Dive deeper.  Beware of your oxygen and battery levels.You keep your upgrades if you drown.

game.short_text: Cosmic Horror Point & Click
game.title: Blood Engine

details.title: Blood Engine
details.description:
A cosmic horror point and click inspired by the movies
Made in one week by a solo dev for the Brackeys Game Jam 2025.2. It is designed to be played embedded in browser (sorry, no fullscreen or executables, but you'll see what I mean!)
I'd really like to expand on it after the jam; more branching narratives, more elaborate puzzles, etc. If you love/hate it please let me know! I'd really love to know:
does it play as you would expect for a game of this format?
is it let down by anything in particular?
is there anything you enjoyed in particular?
is this something you would be interested in playing?
and is this worth continuing? ^_^

Many thanks in advance!



WOW! Can't believe Blood Engine came 40th out of 2,300 entries. Thank you everyone who played and rated, and massive congratulations to everyone who entered. Just completing a game in a week is a fantastic achievement!


Credits
Huge thank you to my niece, Robyn Handley, who recorded the voice lines for the cinematic.
Tools
Made using the Adventure Creator Toolkit for Unity. I wanted to do this project just so I could learn how to use it. :)  Should be useful for fast prototyping in future.
3D Assets
Zerin Labs Low Poly Pack SciFi (I assembled the scene using these assets, took screenshots for the backgrounds and modified the with Gimp.)
2D artwork, animations and VFX are my own.
